name: scaffold-vscode
description: Scaffold is a local-first VS Code extension to turn project context into PRDs, engineering plans, and AI-ready build prompts.

view/title.when: view == scaffold.projects || view == scaffold.knowledgeBase || view == scaffold.prd || view == scaffold.design || view == scaffold.engineeringPlan || view == scaffold.readyToBuild || view == scaffold.build

scaffold.gateMode.description: Gate progression mode. strict enforces sequential approvals; flexible allows manual overrides.

scaffold.dataFolder.description: Folder at workspace root used for Scaffold local data.

scaffold.readyToBuildPromptTemplate.markdownDescription: Template used by **Generate Ready-to-Build Prompt**. Supported tokens: `{{projectName}}`, `{{projectId}}`, `{{knowledgeBasePath}}`, `{{prdPath}}`, `{{designPath}}`, `{{engineeringPlanPath}}`, `{{readyToBuildPath}}`, `{{buildPath}}`, `{{knowledgeBaseIndexPath}}`, `{{prdIndexPath}}`, `{{designIndexPath}}`, `{{engineeringPlanIndexPath}}`, `{{readyToBuildIndexPath}}`, `{{buildIndexPath}}`.

scaffold.readyToBuildPromptOutput.description: Where generated Ready-to-Build prompts are sent.

scaffold.buildPromptTemplate.markdownDescription: Template used by **Generate Build Coding Prompt**. Supported tokens: `{{projectName}}`, `{{projectId}}`, `{{knowledgeBasePath}}`, `{{prdPath}}`, `{{designPath}}`, `{{engineeringPlanPath}}`, `{{readyToBuildPath}}`, `{{buildPath}}`, `{{knowledgeBaseIndexPath}}`, `{{prdIndexPath}}`, `{{designIndexPath}}`, `{{engineeringPlanIndexPath}}`, `{{readyToBuildIndexPath}}`, `{{buildIndexPath}}`.

scaffold.buildPromptOutput.description: Where generated Build coding prompts are sent.

scaffold.onboardPromptTemplate.default:
You are onboarding an existing codebase into Scaffold project sections.
Thoroughly analyze the entire codebase and produce a structured output folder that can be imported into Scaffold as-is.

=== WORKSPACE CONTEXT ===
- Workspace root : {{workspaceRootPath}}
- Project id     : {{projectId}}
- Project name   : {{projectName}}

=== OUTPUT FOLDER STRUCTURE ===
Create one subfolder per section using EXACTLY these folder names:
  {{knowledgeBasePath}}
  {{prdPath}}
  {{designPath}}
  {{engineeringPlanPath}}
  {{readyToBuildPath}}
  {{buildPath}}

=== SECTION DETAILS ===

--- 1. knowledge-base ---
PURPOSE : Reference documentation and background knowledge about the existing system.
ANALYZE : README files, wikis, inline code comments, existing docs/, architecture notes, environment/config files (.env.example, docker-compose, CI configs), dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pom.xml).
PRODUCE :
  - overview.md          : High-level description of what the system is, who uses it, core value.
  - tech-stack.md        : Languages, frameworks, major libraries with versions.
  - architecture.md      : Component/service map, data flow, external integrations.
  - data-model.md        : Key entities, relationships, DB schema summary.
  - environment.md       : Environment variables, secrets, infrastructure requirements.
  - dependencies.md      : Notable third-party dependencies and their role.
  - conventions.md       : Coding conventions, naming patterns, folder structure rules.
  - glossary.md          : Domain-specific terms and abbreviations found in the code.
FILE FORMAT: .md (Markdown). Use headings, tables, and code blocks where helpful.

--- 2. prd ---
PURPOSE : Product Requirement Document capturing what the product must do.
ANALYZE : Feature code, route handlers, UI screens, user-facing API endpoints, test descriptions, issue tracker references in comments, changelog entries.
PRODUCE :
  - product-overview.md  : Vision, goals, target users, success metrics.
  - features.md          : Exhaustive feature list grouped by domain. Each feature: name, description, user story, acceptance criteria.
  - user-flows.md        : Step-by-step user journeys for key workflows.
  - non-functional.md    : Performance, security, accessibility, compliance requirements inferred from codebase.
  - out-of-scope.md      : Capabilities NOT present; gaps you observe.
FILE FORMAT: .md only. Use numbered lists and tables for requirements.

--- 3. design ---
PURPOSE : Visual/UI design artifacts and component specifications.
ANALYZE : HTML templates, JSX/TSX component files, CSS/Tailwind classes, design tokens, theme files, Storybook stories, image assets.
PRODUCE :
  - component-inventory.html : Styled HTML page listing every UI component: name, file path, props/variants.
  - color-typography.html    : Design tokens rendered as visual swatches: colors, fonts, spacing scale.
  - layout-patterns.html     : Grid systems, responsive breakpoints, common layout shells with annotated mockups.
  - ui-states.html           : Loading, empty, error, and success states per key component.
FILE FORMAT: .html only. Every file must be a complete self-contained HTML5 document with inline CSS. No external CDN links.

--- 4. engineering-plan ---
PURPOSE : Technical implementation plan describing how the system is built.
ANALYZE : Source folder structure, module boundaries, API contracts, test suite layout, CI/CD configs, migration files, infrastructure-as-code.
PRODUCE :
  - system-design.md       : Component design: classes, modules, interfaces, DB tables, API routes with method and payload.
  - data-flow.md           : How data moves through the system end-to-end per feature.
  - api-contracts.md       : All API endpoints: path, method, request schema, response schema, auth requirements.
  - infrastructure.md      : Deployment topology, cloud services, container setup, environment tiers.
  - testing-strategy.md    : Existing test coverage, frameworks, what to add next.
  - tech-debt.md           : Known hacks, TODOs, deprecated patterns, security issues.
  - migration-plan.md      : DB migrations present; suggested future migration steps.
  - sequence-diagrams.puml : PlantUML sequence diagrams for the top 3-5 critical flows.
FILE FORMAT: .md for prose, .puml for diagrams. Use code blocks for schemas and examples.

--- 5. ready-to-build ---
PURPOSE : Prioritized task list of planned tasks. Left EMPTY during onboarding.
PRODUCE : Nothing. Do not create any files in this folder.
REASON  : No tasks have been planned yet for this project. The team will populate this section manually after reviewing the knowledge-base, prd, design, and engineering-plan artifacts.

--- 6. build ---
PURPOSE : The complete existing codebase copied verbatim — this is the working source of truth.
ANALYZE : Every source file in the existing project.
PRODUCE :
  - Copy the ENTIRE codebase AS-IS into the build/ subfolder, preserving the full original directory structure and all file names exactly.
  - Do NOT modify, summarise, or filter any source file — copy byte-for-byte.
  - Preserve ALL file extensions exactly as they are in the original project.
  - The only files to OMIT are: node_modules/, .git/, build/dist/out output dirs, compiled artefacts (.class, .pyc, __pycache__), and binary/media assets (.png, .jpg, .gif, .svg, .ico, .woff, .ttf, .mp4, .zip, .tar).
  - DO include: all source code, config files, scripts, SQL, Dockerfiles, CI configs, .env.example, lock files (package-lock.json, pnpm-lock.yaml, etc.).
  - Add a build-notes.md at the root of build/ listing: total files copied, any files intentionally omitted and why, and the original project root path.
FILE FORMAT: every file keeps its original extension. build-notes.md in Markdown.

=== GLOBAL RULES ===
1. Use EXACT subfolder names: knowledge-base, prd, design, engineering-plan, ready-to-build, build.
2. Every .md file must start with a # H1 heading matching the file purpose.
3. Every .html file must be a complete self-contained HTML5 document.
4. Every .puml file must start with @startuml and end with @enduml.
5. Never use placeholder text. If information cannot be found, write: Not found in codebase.
6. Be thorough. More detail is better than less.
7. Cross-reference between files where relevant (e.g. see prd/features.md).

=== OUTPUT REQUIREMENT ===
Produce a single local folder containing exactly the six section subfolders above with all files populated.
When done, provide the absolute path to that folder so it can be imported into Scaffold.
scaffold.onboardPromptTemplate.markdownDescription: Template used by **Onboard Existing Project**. Supported tokens: `{{workspaceRootPath}}`, `{{projectName}}`, `{{projectId}}`, `{{knowledgeBasePath}}`, `{{prdPath}}`, `{{designPath}}`, `{{engineeringPlanPath}}`, `{{readyToBuildPath}}`, `{{buildPath}}`.

scaffold.onboardPromptOutput.description: Where generated onboarding prompts are sent.
